category_title: Elementary English language learners using gesture to mediate mathematical meaning-making
minimal_context: Liliana is working at her desk on a paper and pencil math activity using a data chart titled 'Hands and Fingers'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at the data chart on your paper. Can you identify the title of the problem?

STUDENT: (Looks at the chart but is unable to recognize and state the title)

TUTOR: Let's move on to answering the math problem itself. What is your answer?

STUDENT: (Puts LH palm up, fingers extended, over the paper on the desk with the RH holding the pencil. Looks at palm of hand with fingers extended) One.

TUTOR: You have to be able to explain to me why you said that.

STUDENT: (Shrugs shoulders up and down, with LH still up in the air with elbow resting on desk. Looks at hand, LH palm rotated out over desk and then opens palm towards her face) Because the number... mmmm, I don’t know.

options.A: Get up and move with the student from the desk to the chart on the whiteboard.
options.B: Respond by saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.C: Allow the student to withdraw from the desk for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
options.D: Assign math activities the student has already mastered, as opposed to novel data chart assignments.
